reasoning: The Lean statement quantifies over an arbitrary index type ι and an arbitrary family u : ι → Submodule F V, faithfully capturing "any collection of subspaces." The conclusion "the intersection is a subspace" is expressed as "there exists a submodule U whose carrier equals the intersection of the carriers," which is the standard and logically equivalent way to express that a set forms a subspace. The ambient setup (Field F, AddCommGroup V, Module F V) correctly encodes "vector space V over F." Even the edge case of an empty collection is handled correctly, since the empty intersection equals Set.univ = ↑⊤. No hypotheses are silently added or removed, and the quantifier structure matches the informal claim.
